qualifiedClaim.summary: ARC ships a cryptographically signed, fail-closed governance kernel and a bounded protocol-aware cross-protocol execution fabric across HTTP APIs, MCP, OpenAI tool execution, A2A skills, and ACP capabilities.
constraints: Only the supported authoritative paths are claim-eligible.
Lifecycle and bridge-fidelity limits remain explicit on published surfaces, including the deferred-task A2A/ACP authoritative lifecycle path.
Compatibility helpers are non-authoritative and non-claim-eligible.
Protocol selection is explicit and registry-backed for supported targets, but the claim does not imply a universal multi-hop fabric or proved market-control position.

nonQualifiedClaims: technical control-plane qualification without also running the universal control-plane gate
remainingBlockers: This matrix is intentionally the lower bounded-runtime gate and does not by itself prove the stronger technical control-plane thesis.
The stronger technical control-plane claim is owned by the successor gate in ARC_UNIVERSAL_CONTROL_PLANE_QUALIFICATION_MATRIX.json.
The strategic market-position thesis still lacks ecosystem-scale operational proof beyond the qualified local/runtime evidence lane.

gateConditions.summary: CrossProtocolOrchestrator resolves authoritative non-native targets through an explicit executor seam rather than collapsing every path to Native.
At least one authoritative bridge path executes protocol-to-protocol without terminating immediately in DiscoveryProtocol::Native.
Bridge execution preserves attenuation, trace lineage, fidelity semantics, and receipt references across the authoritative path.
arc-api-protect routes HTTP authorization through the shared embedded kernel-backed authority path.
arc-tower uses the same shared kernel-backed HTTP authority/evidence path as arc-api-protect.
Representative SDK substrates expose governed receipts truthfully and surface degraded fail-open behavior as explicit allow_without_receipt state rather than synthetic receipts.
The supported HTTP/API surfaces can now be described literally as sharing one kernel-backed authority story.
A2A authoritative mediation now exposes blocking send plus deferred-task stream/get/cancel lifecycle semantics without overstating push-stream behavior.
ACP authoritative mediation now exposes preview plus blocking invoke and deferred-task stream/cancel/resume lifecycle semantics on the authoritative path.
Compatibility helpers are isolated from the default authoritative claim surface.
The planning stack agrees on active milestone, latest completed milestone, and execution state.
Early and mid-v3 summary tables no longer describe locally implemented work as blanket Planned placeholders.
Late-v3 requirements, summaries, and milestone audits reconcile to the same implementation truth.
v3.12, v3.13, and v3.14 are archived locally with snapshot artifacts and archived phase directories.
Qualification evidence covers the authoritative protocol fabric, unified HTTP authority, and representative SDK surfaces without overstating unsupported paths.
commands: cargo test -p arc-cross-protocol -p arc-mcp-edge -p arc-acp-edge -p arc-a2a-edge

gateConditions.summary: Protocol, release, standards, and top-level vision docs make the same strongest honest claim.
The repo records one authoritative claim-gate decision and names the exact remaining blockers when the stronger claim is not yet qualified.
Economic/control-plane language is bounded to strategic thesis rather than implied as current shipped proof.
